commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=41e0a9756e36ec2cc9106260121406a47792a35c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR narrows memory-core scoped timeout cleanup so active-memory timeouts preserve the shared QMD manager while still releasing local embedding index managers, and updates the cache regression test.

PR surface: Source -6, Tests -5. Total -11 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: active-memory timeout cleanup routes into closeMemorySearchManager, and current main/release close the cached shared QMD wrapper there. I did not run a live QMD gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer-visible plugin API direction and contributor real behavior proof, so this should not be routed to an automated repair lane from this read-only review.

target.title: fix(memory): keep shared QMD manager alive on active-memory recall timeout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

After an `active-memory` embedded recall run times out, chat-session `memory_search` starts returning `"memory search manager is closed"` even though the QMD backend is healthy and `openclaw memory search --agent main` succeeds against the same backend. The first closed-manager result can also enter the 60s `memory_search` cooldown, so recovery looks sticky until the cooldown expires or the gateway restarts.

Root cause: active-memory's timeout cleanup calls `closeActiveMemorySearchManager({ cfg, agentId })`, which routes to `closeMemorySearchManager` in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts`. That function deleted and closed the **agent-scoped shared QMD full manager** — the same long-lived manager that chat `memory_search` and the startup timers/watchers reuse. A request-scoped recall timeout therefore tore down a shared, healthy resource.

Fixes #96455.

## Why This Change Was Made

The cleanup was added in #84048 to release disposable **local embedding providers** that a hung recall can leave open (node-llama contexts/models that don't always honor an abort signal). That teardown is done by `closeMemoryIndexManagersForAgent`. Closing the cached QMD full manager was collateral and unnecessary for that goal:

- QMD's per-query work is request-scoped and already cancelled by the embedded run abort signal.
- The QMD full manager is long-lived and shared (chat search, startup watchers/intervals via `purpose: "default"`).
- `FallbackMemoryManager.search` already documents the canonical invariant: request-scoped cancellation must keep the shared manager active for concurrent and later searches.

So `closeMemorySearchManager` now releases only the disposable local embedding providers and preserves the live cached QMD manager. In a pure QMD setup there are no builtin index managers cached, so the embedding-provider release is a no-op; the original #84048 builtin/local-embedding teardown path is unchanged (no QMD manager is cached there).

## User Impact

Users with `active-memory` enabled and a QMD-backed `memory_search` no longer lose chat memory recall after an active-memory timeout. Chat `memory_search` keeps using the healthy shared QMD manager instead of surfacing `"memory search manager is closed"` and falling into the 60s cooldown. The local-embedding-provider teardown from #84048 is preserved, so hung recall resources are still released.

## Evidence

Narrow regression test in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts` asserts the new contract: after `closeMemorySearchManager`, the shared QMD manager is **not** closed, the disposable index managers are still released, and a subsequent `getMemorySearchManager` reuses the same live instance.

Before/after (negative control) — same updated test run against the unfixed source vs. the fix:

```text
# Unfixed source (manager torn down on scoped teardown):
 × preserves the shared qmd manager on scoped teardown so chat search keeps working
   AssertionError: expected "vi.fn()" [mockPrimary.close] to not be called at all, but actually been called 1 times
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 35 passed (36)

# With this fix:
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  36 passed (36)
```

Active-memory plugin suite stays green (timeout cleanup still invokes the release path):

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  152 passed (152)
```

`oxfmt --check` clean on both changed files. AI-assisted.
